attempts.code_tried: apply (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction …).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective; rw [leftAdjointUniq_inv_app]; simp only [homEquiv_unit]; refine Eq.trans (unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ hadj M.val) ?_; have hII := unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ M; have key := (map_comp).symm.trans …; have hII2 := …; rw [show hadj.unit.app M.val = forget ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M) from rfl, hII2]; refine Eq.trans (Functor.map_comp (forget) …) ?_; refine Eq.trans (congrArg … (sheafPullbackUnit_forget_eq f M)) ?_; exact Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _) (congrArg …)

attempts.insight: Crux is the forget-transport of the SHEAF-level unit_leftAdjointUniq triangle (restrictFunctorIsoPullback), NOT a B2-style conjugate telescope. Every category-lemma step must be term-mode (Eq.trans/congrArg/:=); rw of category lemmas always misses the SheafOfModules seam. Adjunction.eq_unit_comp_map_iff is the clean transposer (avoids rw [← homEquiv_unit] higher-order-unification failure). Functor.map_comp/map_id need CategoryTheory. qualification (clash with lawful-functor).
